project.title: Proteome of hemolymph in Haemaphysalis flava
project.projectDescription: The proteins of hemolymph proteome coupled with transcriptome, has provided a feasible strategy for the promotion of protein identification and the search for clues to control of tick infestations.
project.sampleProcessingProtocol: Hemolymph from adult engorged H.flava females was collected by leg amputation from Erinaceus europaeus host. Hemolymph proteins were extracted by filter aided sample preparation protocol, digested by trypsin, and assayed by liquid chromatography-tandem mass spectrometry.
project.dataProcessingProtocol: MS data were handed by MaxQuant software (version 1.6.14.0., https://maxquant.net/maxquant/). The MS/MS raw files were searched against the self-constructed H. flava protein database deduced from transcriptomic datasfor identifications of tick proteins. An initial search was set at a precursor quality window of 6 ppm. The search followed the enzymatic cleavage rule of Trypsin/P and allowed maximal two missed cleavage sites and a mass tolerance of 20 ppm for fragment ions. Carbamidomethylation of cysteines was defined as a fixed modification, while protein N-terminal acetylation and methionine oxidation were defined as variable modifications. The cutoff of the global false discovery rate for peptide and protein identification was set to 0.01. Protein abundance was calculated on the basis of the normalized spectral protein intensity.

references.referenceLine: Li D, Liu L, Liu ZL, Tian Y, Gao X, Cheng TY. What are the main proteins in the hemolymph of Haemaphysalis flava ticks?  Front Vet Sci. 2024 11:1387719

Characteristics[DevelopmentalStage]: fully engorged females (300–350 mg in weight)
Characteristics[NumberOfSamples]: 50 ticks were immobilized on a sterile glass slide with tape.
Comment[Instrument]: MS analyses were carried out on a Q Exactive mass spectrometer coupled to Easy nLC (Thermo Fisher Scientific, Waltham, MA, United States) following the same conditions previously described.
Comment[NumberOfMissedCleavages]: allowed a maximum of two missed cleavage sites
